rulings.Case Title: MARGARET CAROL RYDER VS. SATEL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Imposing Third Party Liability On Brewbilt Brewing Company And Assignement Of Director'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 26, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFF MARGARET RYDER'S Motion For An Order Imposing Third Party Liability On Brewbilt Brewing Company And Assignement Of Director'S Fees. Judgment creditor Margaret Carol Ryder's motion for an order imposing third-party liability on BrewBilt Brewing Company, and for an order assigning payments from BrewBilt to satisfy the judgment, is granted in part. BrewBilt is liable to Ryder in the amount of $4,166.67 pursuant to Sec. 706.154. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 18, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN TUCKER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER SEALING PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PORTIONS THEREOF
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 26, 2026, line 14. HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER SEALING PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PORTIONS THEREOF. Plaintiff's sealing motion is granted. Plaintiff inadvertently included in his summary judgment opposition some information that the City and County of San Francisco had clawed back in discovery. Plaintiff filed amended versions of the filings. The City did not oppose sealing the filings containing clawed-back information, but objected to Plaintiff's amendments, contending they were substantive and did not merely remove the clawed-back information. By companion order today, on the City's motion for summary adjudication, the court has exercised its discretion to consider Plaintiff's late, amended filings. The court grants the motion to seal. The documents sealed are set out below. The court does not make Rule 2.550 findings because these documents have been withdrawn as a basis for adjudication. Ordinarily, the court would strike documents that are withdrawn as a basis for adjudication. Here, however, the court seals them to preserve for appellate review the City's argument that the amended documents should not have been considered. [Moving party to provide a list of documents to be sealed by exact title, date of filing, and transaction number.] If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 10, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: ANDRE WILLIAMS ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY 1815 EGBERT AVE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses From Plaintiff Timojene Watson; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 26, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT HOUSING AUTHORITY OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Responses From Plaintiff Timojene Watson. The unopposed motion to compel responses to form interrogatory 17.1 from plaintiff Timojene Watson is granted. By denying some matters set out in the requests for admission, Watson was obliged to explain each of her denials or partial denials under 17.1, which states "Is your response to each request for admission served with these interrogatories an unqualified admission? If not, for each response that is not an unqualified admission: (a) state the number of the request; (b) state all facts upon which you base your response; (c) state the names, ADDRESSES, and telephone numbers of all PERSONS who have knowledge of those facts; and (d) identify all DOCUMENTS and other tangible things that support your response and state the name, ADDRESS, and telephone number of the PERSON who has each DOCUMENT or thing By failing to respond timely to the interrogatory, plaintiff has waived objections. Plaintiff Watson shall provide complete, verified, objection-free responses by March 19, 2026. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 10, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: CEDAR MCREE VS. OSCAR JAHIR HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for February 26, 2026, line 9. DEFENDANTS OSCAR HERNANDEZ, JOSE HERNANDEZ, MARIA DOMINGUEZ'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's first amended complaint is overruled. A demurrer tests whether the challenged cause of action necessarily fails to state a claim for relief. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) The court does not assess the plausibility of plaintiff's facts on demurrer. Here, the accident occurred on or about April 16, 2023. (FAC, para. 21.) Plaintiff alleges the ultimate fact that "the full extent of Plaintiffs' injuries, and the cause thereof, were not discovered, and could not reasonably have been discovered, until on or after September 2, 2023 . . . ." (Id., para. 22.) There is no particularity required for pleading delayed discovery. Accepting that fact as true, plaintiff has alleged that the accrual of the limitations period was tolled through September 2023, less than two years before the complaint was first filed in August 2025. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 16, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
